conditions: Coronary artery disease (history of prior cardiac event)
medications: Nitroglycerin sublingual (as needed for chest pain)
health.mental_health: Since immigrating he has had persistent adjustment stress and insomnia, with occasional anxiety around finances and navigating the U.S. healthcare system; he is considering short-term counseling through the university clinic but worries it could affect perceptions of his reliability.
recent_visits: Cardiology follow-up at St. David's Medical Center for post-event monitoring and medication review (last 2 months)
Nephrology consult at Austin Regional Clinic to establish CKD baseline labs and dietary guidance (last 3 months)
Endocrinology/diabetes education visit through UT Health Austin; A1C review and CGM discussion (last 6 weeks)
Eye exam for diabetic retinopathy screening at a local optometry clinic (last 4 months)

finance.debt: Carrying approximately $9k in relocation and immigration-related expenses on a credit card; also repaying a small family loan used for initial deposits and moving costs. He avoids new debt because of medical co-pays and uncertainty about future work authorization paperwork timelines.
investments: Small checking/savings balance at a U.S. credit union
Modest cash savings held in a foreign bank account
No U.S. retirement account yet (planning to open an IRA once settled)
recent_transactions: Paid SEVIS fee and immigration document processing costs
First-month rent and security deposit for an apartment near North Lamar
Out-of-pocket copay for cardiology visit and lab work
Purchased a used blood pressure monitor and glucometer supplies
International money transfer to support a sibling temporarily back home

relationships: Spouse: Rania Al-Rashid (currently abroad; long-distance while paperwork and finances stabilize)
Adult daughter: Lina (living in Houston; provides practical help navigating U.S. systems)
Lab mate: Miguel Herrera (shares tips on campus resources and affordable groceries)
Community contact: Imam Kareem at a North Austin mosque (helps him connect with local immigrant community)
Neighbor: Mrs. Janet Collins (checks in occasionally; friendly but he keeps details private)
social.social_media_activity: Uses WhatsApp daily with family overseas and a small Telegram group for diaspora community updates; maintains a low-profile Facebook account mainly to follow university announcements and local events, rarely posts personal details.
private_conversations: Discussing the strain of long-distance marriage and whether his spouse should delay moving due to his health and finances
Seeking advice about immigration paperwork timelines and fear of jeopardizing status if he reduces course load for medical reasons
Confiding to his daughter about worry after occasional chest tightness and his reluctance to burden family
Talking with the imam about loneliness and finding culturally familiar support networks

schedule.work_hours: Typically 9:30am–6:30pm on weekdays split between lab work, reading, and teaching assistant duties; lighter hours on Fridays to accommodate medical appointments; weekends include 3–5 hours of writing plus errands.
regular_appointments: Biweekly therapy intake waitlist check-in with the university counseling center (phone)
Monthly cardiology follow-up or remote check-in depending on symptoms
Quarterly A1C/labs (diabetes and kidney function panel)
upcoming_events: Nephrology follow-up to review eGFR and urine albumin results (next month)
Department seminar where he is expected to network with visiting faculty (in 2 weeks)
International student services appointment to confirm document updates (next week)
Zoom call with spouse to review moving plans and budget (Sunday evening)
schedule.daily_routine: Morning glucose check, short walk when weather allows, campus shuttle to UT, structured work blocks with a mid-day low-sodium lunch, evening medication routine, and a nightly call with family overseas when time zones align.

professional.company: The University of Texas at Austin (public research university; department-based assistantship)
projects: Dissertation research on privacy-preserving data analysis methods for health datasets
Teaching assistant responsibilities for an undergraduate data science course (grading and office hours)
Collaborative paper draft with a postdoc on differential privacy evaluation metrics
Reproducibility work: cleaning and documenting code for a lab open-source release
professional.performance_issues: He is behind on one conference submission due to fatigue and multiple specialist visits; he worries that requesting formal accommodations could affect his standing or funding, and he sometimes avoids disclosing health issues to his advisor.
workplace_relationships: Advisor: Prof. Elaine Whitaker (supportive but expects consistent progress; Pavel is cautious about sharing medical details)
Department admin: Shannon Price (helps with payroll/insurance; Pavel relies on her for navigating benefits)
Peer dynamic: a younger cohort is fast-paced; Pavel feels pressure and occasional imposter syndrome as an older student and recent immigrant

hobbies: Long walks on the Ann and Roy Butler Hike-and-Bike Trail (when energy allows)
Cooking traditional dishes adapted for low-sodium and lower-carb needs
Listening to Arabic-language news and podcasts
Reading history and biographies in the evenings
Attending occasional community lectures and museum events (Blanton Museum of Art)
habits: Checks blood glucose most mornings and logs readings
Avoids alcohol and limits caffeine after noon due to palpitations and sleep issues
Uses a pill organizer and sets phone alarms for medications
Occasionally skips social events to manage fatigue and protect privacy about his health
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a modest one-bedroom apartment in North Austin near a bus route to campus; living alone while his spouse remains abroad, keeping expenses tight and prioritizing proximity to medical clinics and groceries.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Follows a diabetes- and kidney-friendly approach: reduced refined carbs, careful portion sizes, low sodium, moderate protein, and avoids high-potassium foods when labs trend high; prefers home-cooked meals and reads nutrition labels closely.
